presets.description: Calm, approachable helper — cool blue turtleneck harmonized with the purple hair.
High-energy mascot — sunny hair + coral tee for a warm, cheerful scheme.
Composed and sharp — steel-blue hair and a slate turtleneck, monochrome cool palette.
Cozy and laid-back — droopy eyes, cat mouth, and a soft teal hoodie.

  <title>Kugutu Mascot Rig</title>
  <!--
    Unified-art-direction rig. Face/hair/feature parts are file fragments drawn
    around their own local origin and injected at the anchor mounts below
    (data-kugutu-slot-mount). Paired slots (eye, brow) have a left mount and a
    mirrored right mount, so a single part fragment fills both sides.

    Palette tokens (apply consistently across all part fragments):
      skin        #FAD0B0    skin-shade  #F3B591
      ink         #34344A    blush       #FF9E9E
      hair        #7C6CF0    hair-shade  #6253D6
      accent      #FF8360    accent-2    #46C2A8
      white       #FFFFFF
  -->

  <ellipse cx="160" cy="296" rx="92" ry="15" fill="#34344A" opacity="0.10" />

  <!--
    Arms are FK joint chains, but the joints are NOT DOM-nested: the runtime
    composes each joint's transform from its <circle data-kugutu-pivot> marker
    (shoulder/elbow/wrist, in root coords) and the slot parent chain. That lets
    the joints live at different z-layers: the UPPER ARMS are drawn BEFORE the
    torso (behind the shirt) while the FOREARMS + HANDS are drawn AFTER it (in
    front). So the shoulder/upper-arm tucks behind the clothes and the forearm
    reads in front — without breaking the shoulder→elbow→wrist bend.

    Single-tone skin capsules (round-capped <line>s overlapping at the elbow so
    they read as one soft, bendable arm) with mitten hands. Rest pose is a
    relaxed V bend with the hands held up at the sides, kept above the outfit
    hem so nothing dangles below the body.
  -->
  <g id="upper_arm_l">
    <circle data-kugutu-pivot cx="120" cy="240" r="0.01" fill="none" />
    <line x1="120" y1="240" x2="82" y2="266" stroke="#FAD0B0" stroke-width="18" stroke-linecap="round" />
  </g>
  <g id="upper_arm_r">
    <circle data-kugutu-pivot cx="200" cy="240" r="0.01" fill="none" />
    <line x1="200" y1="240" x2="238" y2="266" stroke="#FAD0B0" stroke-width="18" stroke-linecap="round" />
  </g>

  <g id="torso_group">
    <g transform="translate(160 250)">
      <g data-kugutu-slot-mount="outfit"><g data-kugutu-variant-slot="outfit" data-kugutu-variant-id="outfit-tee"><g>
  <!-- Tee body: gently tapered, soft rounded hem. -->
  <path d="M-60 -18 Q-32 -34 0 -34 Q32 -34 60 -18 L68 36 Q0 46 -68 36 Z" fill="#FF8360" />
  <!-- Neckline (a soft collar accent). -->
  <path d="M-21 -31 Q0 -15 21 -31" fill="none" stroke="#E96A4A" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" />
  <!-- Short cap sleeves = the shirt's shoulder. Each is wrapped in a follower
       group so it rotates with its upper arm (data-kugutu-follow), pivoting at
       the shoulder (outfit-local: root shoulder 200/120,240 minus the mount's
       translate 160,250 = ±40,-10). amount<1 = fabric lag. The darker cuff band
       marks the sleeve opening so it reads as a rolled sleeve, not a wrinkle. -->
  <g data-kugutu-follow="upperArm.r" data-kugutu-follow-amount="0.85">
    <circle data-kugutu-pivot cx="40" cy="-10" r="0.01" fill="none" />

  </g>

<svg
  xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
  viewBox="0 0 320 320"
  fill="none"
  role="img"
>
  <title>Kugutu Mascot Rig</title>
  <!--
    Unified-art-direction rig. Face/hair/feature parts are file fragments drawn
    around their own local origin and injected at the anchor mounts below
    (data-kugutu-slot-mount). Paired slots (eye, brow) have a left mount and a
    mirrored right mount, so a single part fragment fills both sides.

    Palette tokens (apply consistently across all part fragments):
      skin        #FAD0B0    skin-shade  #F3B591
      ink         #34344A    blush       #FF9E9E
      hair        #7C6CF0    hair-shade  #6253D6
      accent      #FF8360    accent-2    #46C2A8
      white       #FFFFFF
  -->

  <ellipse cx="160" cy="296" rx="92" ry="15" fill="#34344A" opacity="0.10" />

  <!--
    Arms are FK joint chains, but the joints are NOT DOM-nested: the runtime
    composes each joint's transform from its <circle data-kugutu-pivot> marker
    (shoulder/elbow/wrist, in root coords) and the slot parent chain. That lets
    the joints live at different z-layers: the UPPER ARMS are drawn BEFORE the
    torso (behind the shirt) while the FOREARMS + HANDS are drawn AFTER it (in
    front). So the shoulder/upper-arm tucks behind the clothes and the forearm
    reads in front — without breaking the shoulder→elbow→wrist bend.

    Single-tone skin capsules (round-capped <line>s overlapping at the elbow so
    they read as one soft, bendable arm) with mitten hands. Rest pose is a
    relaxed V bend with the hands held up at the sides, kept above the outfit
    hem so nothing dangles below the body.
  -->
  <g id="upper_arm_l">
    <circle data-kugutu-pivot cx="120" cy="240" r="0.01" fill="none" />
    <line x1="120" y1="240" x2="82" y2="266" stroke="#FAD0B0" stroke-width="18" stroke-linecap="round" />
  </g>
  <g id="upper_arm_r">
    <circle data-kugutu-pivot cx="200" cy="240" r="0.01" fill="none" />
    <line x1="200" y1="240" x2="238" y2="266" stroke="#FAD0B0" stroke-width="18" stroke-linecap="round" />
  </g>

  <g id="torso_group">
    <g transform="translate(160 250)">
      <g data-kugutu-slot-mount="outfit"></g>
    </g>
  </g>

  <!-- The neck is sandwiched between an outfit's base/rear artwork and its
       optional front layer, so collars can wrap around it naturally. -->
  <g id="neck_group">
    <rect x="146" y="192" width="28" height="30" rx="12" fill="#F3B591" />
    <g transform="translate(160 250)">
      <g data-kugutu-slot-mount="outfit" data-kugutu-slot-layer="front"></g>
    </g>
  </g>

  <g id="forearm_l">
    <circle data-kugutu-pivot cx="82" cy="266" r="0.01" fill="none" />
    <line x1="82" y1="266" x2="90" y2="232" stroke="#FAD0B0" stroke-width="16" stroke-linecap="round" />
  </g>
  <g id="hand_l">
    <circle data-kugutu-pivot cx="90" cy="232" r="0.01" fill="none" />
    <ellipse cx="88" cy="228" rx="13" ry="14" fill="#FAD0B0" />
  </g>
  <g id="forearm_r">
    <circle data-kugutu-pivot cx="238" cy="266" r="0.01" fill="none" />
    <line x1="238" y1="266" x2="230" y2="232" stroke="#FAD0B0" stroke-width="16" stroke-linecap="round" />
  </g>
  <g id="hand_r">
    <circle data-kugutu-pivot cx="230" cy="232" r="0.01" fill="none" />
    <ellipse cx="232" cy="228" rx="13" ry="14" fill="#FAD0B0" />
  </g>

  <g id="head_group">
    <g transform="translate(160 126)">
      <g id="hair_back">
        <g data-kugutu-slot-mount="hair.back"></g>
      </g>
    </g>

    <g transform="translate(160 126)">
      <g data-kugutu-slot-mount="face"></g>
    </g>

    <g transform="translate(132 104)">
      <g id="brow_left">
        <g data-kugutu-slot-mount="brow"></g>
      </g>
    </g>
    <g transform="translate(188 104)">
      <g id="brow_right">
        <g data-kugutu-slot-mount="brow" transform="scale(-1 1)"></g>
      </g>
    </g>

    <g transform="translate(132 132)">
      <g id="eye_left">
        <g data-kugutu-slot-mount="eye"></g>
      </g>
    </g>
    <g transform="translate(188 132)">
      <g id="eye_right">
        <g data-kugutu-slot-mount="eye" transform="scale(-1 1)"></g>
      </g>
    </g>

    <g transform="translate(160 142)">
      <g id="nose_node">
        <g data-kugutu-slot-mount="nose"></g>
      </g>
    </g>

    <!-- Beard sits under the mouth so lips/teeth stay readable on top of it.
         Anchored at the mouth origin: mustaches draw above y=0, chin beards below. -->
    <g transform="translate(160 170)">
      <g data-kugutu-slot-mount="beard"></g>
    </g>

    <g transform="translate(160 170)">
      <g id="mouth_group">
        <g data-kugutu-slot-mount="mouth"></g>
      </g>
    </g>

    <g transform="translate(160 126)">
      <g id="hair_front">
        <g data-kugutu-slot-mount="hair.front"></g>
      </g>
    </g>

    <!-- Accessories render above the front hair (Mii-style) so they never get
         buried under bangs. Glasses are anchored at the eye line midpoint;
         hair accessories share the head origin like the hair mounts. -->
    <g transform="translate(160 132)">
      <g data-kugutu-slot-mount="glasses"></g>
    </g>

    <g transform="translate(160 126)">
      <g data-kugutu-slot-mount="hair.accessory"></g>
    </g>
  </g>
</svg>

Composed and sharp — steel-blue hair and a slate turtleneck, monochrome cool palette.
Cozy and laid-back — droopy eyes, cat mouth, and a soft teal hoodie.

partAssets.outfit-tee:
<g>
  <!-- Tee body: gently tapered, soft rounded hem. -->
  <path d="M-60 -18 Q-32 -34 0 -34 Q32 -34 60 -18 L68 36 Q0 46 -68 36 Z" fill="#FF8360" />
  <!-- Neckline (a soft collar accent). -->
  <path d="M-21 -31 Q0 -15 21 -31" fill="none" stroke="#E96A4A" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" />
  <!-- Short cap sleeves = the shirt's shoulder. Each is wrapped in a follower
       group so it rotates with its upper arm (data-kugutu-follow), pivoting at
       the shoulder (outfit-local: root shoulder 200/120,240 minus the mount's
       translate 160,250 = ±40,-10). amount<1 = fabric lag. The darker cuff band
       marks the sleeve opening so it reads as a rolled sleeve, not a wrinkle. -->
  <g data-kugutu-follow="upperArm.r" data-kugutu-follow-amount="0.85">
    <circle data-kugutu-pivot cx="40" cy="-10" r="0.01" fill="none" />
    <path d="M40 -22 Q66 -24 74 -2 Q76 13 59 17 Q46 7 42 -10 Z" fill="#FF8360" />
    <path d="M59 16 Q73 11 74 -2" fill="none" stroke="#E96A4A" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" />
  </g>
  <g data-kugutu-follow="upperArm.l" data-kugutu-follow-amount="0.85">
    <circle data-kugutu-pivot cx="-40" cy="-10" r="0.01" fill="none" />
    <path d="M-40 -22 Q-66 -24 -74 -2 Q-76 13 -59 17 Q-46 7 -42 -10 Z" fill="#FF8360" />
    <path d="M-59 16 Q-73 11 -74 -2" fill="none" stroke="#E96A4A" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" />
  </g>
</g>
